Article.ArticleTitle: For a healthy start in life, children need smoke-free environments: Progress of the Generation Smoke-Free campaign in Belgium since its launch in 2018.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Belgian Alliance for Smoke Free Society, Brussels, Belgium.
The Human Rights Centre, Ghent University, Ghent, Belgium.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: The author has completed and submitted an ICMJE form for disclosure of potential conflicts of interest. The author declares that she has no competing interests, financial or otherwise, related to the current work. D.V. Kalmthout reports grants from the Belgian Alliance for a Smoke-Free Society (VZW), and is funded by ‘Kom op tegen Kanker’ and the Foundation Against Cancer.

Reference.Citation: Gisle L, Demarest S, Drieskens S. Gezondheidsenquête 2018: GEBRUIK VAN TABAK. Sciensano; 2019. Rapportnummer D/2019/14.440/57. Accessed February 10, 2022. https://www.sciensano.be/sites/default/files/ta_rapport2_his2018_nl_v3.pdf.

Cellule Générale de Politique . STRATÉGIE INTERFÉDÉRALE 2022-2028 POUR UNE GÉNÉRATION SANS TABAC. Cellule Générale de Politique; 2022. Accessed February 10, 2022. https://organesdeconcertation.sante.belgique.be/sites/default/files/documents/20220323_strategie_interfederale_tabac_note_de_base_fr.pdf.
MPOWER. World Health Organization; Accessed February 10, 2022. https://www.who.int/initiatives/mpower.

ASH Briefing for local authorities: Pavement licences and smoking. Action on Smoking and Health; 2020. Sep, Accessed February 10, 2022. https://ash.org.uk/uploads/Smokefree-pavement-licenses-briefing-September-2020.pdf.

International Agency for Research on Cancer . IARC monographs on the evaluation of carcinogenic risks to humans, volume 100 E. A review of human carcinogens: Personal habits and indoor combustions. IARC; 2012. https://monographs.iarc.fr/wp-content/uploads/2018/06/mono100E.pdf.
World Health Organization . Tobacco. World Health Organization; 2022. May 24, 2022. Accessed February 10, 2022. https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/tobacco.
Samet JM. Tobacco Smoke: Active and Passive Smoking. In: Landrigan PJ, Etzel RA, editors. Textbook of Children's Environmental Health. Oxford University Press; 2013. pp. 222–231.

Evans-Reeves K, Lauber K, Hiscock R. The 'filter fraud' persists: the tobacco industry is still using filters to suggest lower health risks while destroying the environment. Tob Control. 2022;31(e1):e80–e82. doi: 10.1136/tobaccocontrol-2020-056245.

World Health Organization . Tobacco and its environmental impact: an overview. WHO; 2017. Accessed February 10, 2022. https://apps.who.int/iris/bitstream/handle/10665/255574/9789241512497-eng.pdf;jsessionid=A32241439FDF7785772A18BA06604D1A?sequence=1.
